research_summary: As of April 2026, Howick's average house value sits at $1,109,750 — down 3.99% year-on-year and 7% from two years ago, with an average of 37 days to sell. Auckland's median house price is $1,014,000 (Feb 2026), down 21.39% from the Nov 2021 peak. The OCR was cut to 2.25% in late 2025, easing mortgage rates from their highs. Inventory across Auckland remains elevated, firmly a buyer's market. ANZ has revised 2026 price growth forecasts down to just 2%. East Auckland suburbs like Howick, Botany, Pakuranga, Flat Bush and Dannemora are seeing subdued demand, longer days on market, and motivated vendors — creating a rare entry window for buyers.
content_angle: Buyer tip — East Auckland's best buying window in years: prices corrected, rates dropping, and vendors negotiating.
hook: Howick house prices are DOWN almost 4% in a year. The OCR is at 2.25%. If you've been waiting to buy in East Auckland — this might be your moment. 👀
script: Let's talk East Auckland real estate right now. Howick's average home is sitting at $1.1M — that's nearly 4% cheaper than a year ago, and 7% below where it was two years back. Meanwhile the Reserve Bank has cut the OCR to 2.25%, which means mortgage rates have come off their peak. Add in that there are MORE homes for sale than this time last year, and vendors are waiting longer — 37 days on average just in Howick. That's power in your hands as a buyer. Suburbs like Flat Bush, Botany and Pakuranga are showing similar trends. If you've been sitting on the fence, the data says the fence is looking pretty good to jump off — on the buying side. I'm covering East Auckland exclusively, so if you want to know what your budget can actually get you right now, drop me a message and let's talk numbers.
caption: East Auckland property update 🏡 Howick values are down ~4% year-on-year, the OCR just hit 2.25%, and there's more stock on the market than we've seen in years. Vendors are negotiating.
